mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5% in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates a tightening labor market, which accelerates wage pressures and prompts monetary policy hawkishness, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX records positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases where UNRATE declines by > 0.3pp in 1 month, or t-test p-value > 0.1 for mean return difference.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, which drive corporate earnings and stock prices higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 40% of periods where GDPC1 and HOUST both rise by > 1% in 6 months, or interaction term coefficient < 0.1 in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103.0258 indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and drives S&P 500 returns positively over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 5d returns in > 30% of instances where INDPRO rises by > 1% in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 301.808) during elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.65%) creates a stagflationary environment, which depresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both exceed thresholds, or interaction term coefficient > 0.15 in regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) reflects strong household demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives S&P 500 returns positively over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 20d returns in > 40% of cases where RSAFS rises by > 2% in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market, which raises inflation expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS rises by > 100k and UNRATE falls by > 0.2pp, or interaction term coefficient > 0.1 in regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103.0258 signals expanding manufacturing capacity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns positively over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX records negative 60d returns in > 35% of instances where INDPRO rises by > 1% in 1 month, or Rank IC < 0.025 over 2-year window.
